supersedes: v3 anchor (2026-07-14): re-established on the DELEGATED-REQUIRED-ONLY serving path (21f722c, 2026-07-13 — direct-root response signing deleted; the proxy now ALWAYS issues a delegated-credential-backed response signature per request). The prior v2 anchor (5866.4 rps, recorded 2026-07-12) measured the pre-cutover sign_response_full path and is retired: always-delegated-signing costs ~16% throughput (5866.4 -> 4906.9) and ~18% p50 added latency (21450 -> 25334us) on this box, the expected cost of the mandated architecture, not a regression. (The v2 note itself retired the v1 object/JCS carrier baseline.) Same box, same 128/8000/1-core config; the serving path is what changed.
caveat: Recorded on a single developer workstation with the load generator CO-LOCATED with the proxy on the same cores. At the concurrency this anchor uses the run is client-concurrency-bound (128 concurrent cold mTLS handshakes; client-side Ed25519/TLS crypto costs as much as server-side), so throughput is a stable REGRESSION anchor but is NOT a production capacity number, and the 1->N core sweep below is recorded-but-NON-AUTHORITATIVE: on one box the loadgen contends with the fleet for cores and flattens the curve. Authoritative absolute SLOs and the real scaling curve require a dedicated load-generator host driving the proxy on the production hardware class (MCPRE-110 production half; the GKE fleet run, now at the canonical 128/8000 envelope).

recorded.serving_mode: delegated-required-only (ADR-MCPRE-052; delegated-credential-backed response signature per request)
recorded.carrier: RFC 9421 HTTP Message Signatures + RFC 9530 Content-Digest (signature in HTTP headers; no object/JCS _meta signature)

anchor.comment: The regression anchor on the delegated-required serving path: single served core, moderate concurrency (least loadgen-distorted, most reproducible). Values are the MEDIAN of 6 reps on a developer workstation with co-located loadgen (throughput spread 4827.7-5103.5, ~+/-3%; the box is not perfectly quiet, hence 6 reps and slightly wider local_regression tolerances than a dedicated-loadgen run would need). adr051_slo_gate.py compares a fresh run of THIS config against these numbers.

recorded_core_sweep_non_authoritative.comment: PRE-CUTOVER, retained for the record only (measured on the old sign_response_full path, 2026-07-12) and NON-AUTHORITATIVE: throughput is flat-to-noisy as served cores rise because the co-located loadgen is the limiter, not per-core scaling. Do NOT gate on this curve; the authoritative 1->N curve is the GKE fleet run.
